title: Toolbar type selector supports deselected state to show all issue types
description:
Change the toolbar AnimatedSelector (Initiatives / Epics / Issues) so that clicking the currently-selected option deselects it, resulting in a none/unfiltered state where all issue types are visible. Clicking any option re-activates filtering for that type.

Scenario: Clicking the selected option deselects it

Given the type selector currently has Epics selected
When the user clicks Epics again
Then no option is selected (the selector shows a deselected/neutral state)
And the kanban board shows all issue types (initiatives, epics, tasks, bugs, etc.)
And no type filtering is applied

Scenario: Clicking an option when nothing is selected activates filtering

Given the type selector is in the deselected state
When the user clicks Issues
Then Issues becomes selected
And the kanban board filters to show only issue-type items
And initiatives, epics, and sub-tasks are hidden

Scenario: Clicking a different option switches the active filter

Given the type selector has Epics selected
When the user clicks Initiatives
Then Initiatives becomes selected
And Epics is no longer selected
And the board filters to show only initiatives

Scenario: Deselected state is visually distinct

Given the type selector is in the deselected state
Then no option appears highlighted or active
And the selector communicates the neutral/all state visually

Scenario: Deselected state persists across page reload

Given the user has deselected all type filters
When the page is reloaded
Then the type selector remains in the deselected state
And all issue types are shown
creator: ryan.porter
